# Provider Operations & Privacy Checklist: Operational Real Providers

**Purpose**: Validate that provider selection, project-scoped operation, remote privacy controls, rollback, and real-service evidence requirements are complete and implementation-ready
**Created**: 2026-07-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for selecting one concrete local provider and one concrete remote provider, including identity and supported version? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are provider license, redistribution, model/language-resource license, and maintenance-status requirements documented for both selected paths? [Completeness, Gap]
- [x] CHK003 Are all local lifecycle requirements documented for preparation, start, readiness, stop, restart, update, rollback, persistence, cleanup, and complete removal? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005-FR-006, §FR-021-FR-022]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for initial artifact acquisition and integrity verification before offline operation? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for every configuration surface used by the command line and direct Zed workflow, including drift detection between them? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019, §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK006 Are remote account, free-tier, API credential, quota, endpoint, and service-availability requirements documented for the selected provider? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007-FR-008, Clarifications]
- [x] CHK007 Are real-service evidence requirements complete for all four provider/surface combinations and required negative scenarios? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019-FR-020, §SC-001, §SC-011]
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements defined for safe upgrade eligibility, version pinning, compatibility checks, and restoration of the last known-good local provider? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021, User Story 3]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Are `mock/offline`, `local/offline`, and `remote/online` defined consistently by network boundary and user-visible locality rather than provider branding? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK010 Is “project-scoped isolated environment” translated into explicit plan boundaries for runtime, network, filesystem, ports, and persistent artifacts? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Scope Boundaries]
- [x] CHK011 Is the point after which the local provider must operate without Internet access defined unambiguously, including model and image availability? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, §SC-002]
- [x] CHK012 Are readiness and health criteria specific enough to distinguish process startup from an English-to-Spanish-capable provider? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, User Story 3]
- [x] CHK013 Is idempotent or safe-failure behavior defined separately for start, stop, verification, cleanup, and repeated operations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK014 Is “no paid subscription required” distinguished from free account, payment-data, trial, quota, and overage requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Clarifications]
- [x] CHK015 Are “last known-good,” failed update, incompatible artifact, and successful rollback defined with objective entry and exit criteria? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021, §SC-009]
- [x] CHK016 Are safe evidence fields and prohibited evidence content enumerated without relying on vague terms such as “redacted appropriately”? [Clarity, Spec §FR-017, §FR-020]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK017 Are selected provider requirements consistent with `MockProvider` remaining the deterministic default? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, §SC-010]
- [x] CHK018 Are local provider network requirements consistent across initial acquisition, offline runtime, health checks, updates, and rollback? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003-FR-006, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK019 Are remote endpoint and credential requirements consistent with HTTPS allowlisting, no inherited arbitrary configuration, and request-specific confirmation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007-FR-011]
- [x] CHK020 Are provider payload requirements consistent with segmentation, Markdown protection, fixed languages, tone metadata, and forbidden context? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012-FR-014]
- [x] CHK021 Are CLI and direct Zed requirements consistent about provider selection, locality labels, remote confirmation, secret references, and normalized outcomes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009-FR-011, §FR-019]
- [x] CHK022 Are lifecycle and cleanup requirements consistent with persistent artifact preservation and explicit destructive cleanup boundaries? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005-FR-006, User Story 3]
- [x] CHK023 Are all provider requirements consistent with constitutional no-mutation, offline-default, explicit limits, redaction, and minimal-host-footprint gates? [Consistency, Constitution §§I-V, Spec §FR-013-FR-017]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK024 Can all four successful real-service acceptance runs be objectively distinguished from mock or stub output without storing translation content as evidence? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, §SC-011]
- [x] CHK025 Is offline acceptance objectively measurable by an explicit external-network-disabled condition and a defined permitted local boundary? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK026 Are clean-checkout and no-global-installation criteria measurable for the complete local lifecycle? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK027 Can pre-contact denial be objectively demonstrated for confirmation and secret-gate cases without recording prohibited content? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004-FR-005, §FR-017]
## Scenario & Edge-Case Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Are primary requirements complete for local CLI, local Zed, remote CLI, remote Zed, and mock-default journeys? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1-2]
- [x] CHK029 Are exception requirements complete for interrupted/corrupt acquisition, port or instance conflict, readiness failure, mid-request outage, timeout, quota, rejected credential, and invalid response? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-015]
- [x] CHK030 Are recovery requirements complete for failed start, partial update, incompatible model/resource, rollback failure, and explicit destructive cleanup? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK031 Are remote-consent requirements complete for denial, dismissal, stale/mismatched response, prior-consent reuse, and secret detection after confirmation? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010-FR-011, §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK032 Are upstream provider changes covered, including host migration, certificate failure, API-version change, free-tier removal, and terms/privacy-policy change? [Coverage, Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK033 Are expected CPU, memory, disk, download-size, startup-time, and translation-latency envelopes documented for the selected local provider? [Completeness, Gap]
- [x] CHK034 Are supply-chain requirements defined for provider images/packages, model artifacts, pinned versions, digests/checksums, and approved sources? [Security, Gap]
- [x] CHK035 Are remote data-handling, retention, training-use, geographic-processing, and privacy-policy findings documented for informed per-request consent? [Privacy, Gap]
- [x] CHK036 Are observability requirements sufficient to diagnose lifecycle and provider failures while prohibiting content, credentials, sensitive URLs, and paths? [Security, Spec §FR-015, §FR-017]
- [x] CHK037 Are provider timeout, input/segment/output limits, and rate/quota constraints defined without silently weakening existing contracts? [Performance, Spec §FR-013, §FR-015]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK038 Are host prerequisites classified as existing transversal tools or project-scoped dependencies, with no implicit global runtime installation? [Dependency, Spec §FR-003, Scope Boundaries]
- [x] CHK039 Are assumptions about target architecture, provider/model availability, English-to-Spanish support, initial network access, disk capacity, and credential access validated against primary sources? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK040 Are external service availability, quota, account eligibility, and change-control dependencies documented with a safe fallback to mock/local behavior? [Dependency, Spec §FR-002, §FR-006-FR-008]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference

Evaluation completed on 2026-07-14: 40/40 requirement-quality questions are
addressed by `spec.md`, `research.md`, `data-model.md`, the four contracts,
`quickstart.md`, and `manual-validation.md`. CHK002 passes because unresolved
Argos model licensing is explicitly documented as a no-redistribution and
publication gate; it does not mean that upstream licensing has been resolved.
CHK035 passes because the plan records both documented no-persistence and the
absence of an explicit current training-use promise, plus the global
endpoint's lack of geographic residency guarantee.

Implementation review completed on 2026-07-14: the credential-free automatic
gate, focused provider matrix, full workspace regressions, formatting, Clippy
and cargo-deny pass. Controlled tests cover the checklist's lifecycle,
consent, payload, failure, redaction, non-mutation and recovery requirements.
This 40/40 result evaluates requirement quality and automated implementation;
it does not mark the real-service acceptance rows complete. T056 remains
unchecked pending explicit approval, LibreTranslate artifact acquisition, an
eligible Azure F0 resource/key and redacted human review. Argos model licensing
also remains unresolved, so bundling, redistribution and publication stay
blocked even though the checklist requirement to expose that gate is met.
